channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Church of England to Issue Apology for Involvement in Forced Adoptions After WWII
item.description: The Church of England is set to apologise for its involvement in the forced adoption of tens of thousands of babies from unmarried mothers, a practice that spanned three decades following World War II...

item.title: US and Iran Agree on Temporary Ceasefire Following Pakistan-Mediated Talks
item.description: On April 8, 2026, Pakistan played a central role in facilitating a temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran, following direct talks with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Defenc...

item.title: Israel continues military action in Lebanon despite US-brokered ceasefire with Iran
item.description: Israel has confirmed that it will keep up its military operations in Lebanon despite Donald Trump’s recent ceasefire announcement aimed at halting hostilities with Iran. The Israeli government clarifi...

item.title: Edmonton hosts third PWHL Takeover Tour game amid league expansion considerations
item.description: Edmonton welcomed the third stop on the Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) Takeover Tour, showcasing top women's hockey talent to fans in a city passionate about the sport. The event featured a...

item.title: Scottish crime boss Steven Lyons faces extradition after arrest in Bali
item.description: Steven Lyons, a notorious Scottish crime boss, has been extradited following his arrest in Bali. The criminal figure, who was reportedly involved in a series of criminal activities, had been evading j...

item.title: State Witness Testifies P1.72 Billion Went to Former DPWH Engineer for Flood Control Projects
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – During a hearing at the Sandiganbayan on April 8, 2026, state witness and contractor Sally Santos confirmed she allegedly delivered approximately P1.72 billion to former DPWH ass...

item.title: Srinagar Police Uncover Multi-State Lashkar-e-Taiba Network Linked to Terrorist Activities
item.description: Srinagar police have recently uncovered another multi-state network linked to Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), which has been active across Jammu and Kashmir and other states like Haryana and Rajasthan. This in...

item.title: The Importance of Keeping Your Insurance Active During Tough Financial Times
item.description: As the cost of living continues to rise, households are being forced to reconsider their monthly expenses, making it tempting to cut back on non-essential costs. However, experts are warning against s...

item.title: Jumia expands reach to Nigeria’s rural markets to meet rising inland e-commerce demand
item.description: Jumia, one of Nigeria’s foremost e-commerce platforms, don begin expand its operations beyond the big cities like Lagos and Abuja into smaller towns and rural communities. The company dey respond to n...

item.title: BBC criticised for breaching editorial standards in BAFTA coverage involving racial comment
item.description: A BBC investigation has found that the broadcaster failed to meet its own editorial standards in coverage relating to a racial slur during the BAFTA Awards. The internal review, reportedly carried out...

item.title: RUWASA Allocates 15.54bn/- for Key Water Supply Projects in 2026/27
item.description: The Rural Water Supply and Sanitation Agency (RUWASA) has allocated a budget of 15.54bn/- for the 2026/27 financial year to carry out essential water and sanitation projects in Dodoma Region. This bud...

item.title: Kenyan Somalis Face Challenges in Reclaiming Citizenship After Refugee Registration
item.description: In Kenya, thousands of ethnic Somali Kenyans, including children taken to the Dadaab refugee camp, are caught in a legal and bureaucratic limbo. Many were registered as refugees without consent, despi...

item.title: Erratic weather in Kashmir may trigger flash floods and landslides till April 10, says IMD
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow alert for the Kashmir Valley following persistent rainfall and snowfall in various parts of the region. According to the IMD, erratic weat...

item.title: High court rules children may decide burial place of deceased father
item.description: A Johannesburg High Court has ruled that the children of a man originally from Zimbabwe have the right to decide where he should be buried, upholding their wish to lay him to rest in Cape Town, where ...

item.title: Kenyan Parents Share Benefits of Day Schooling Over Boarding
item.description: In Kenya, a growing number of parents are opting for day schooling over traditional boarding schools due to emotional, practical, and developmental reasons. Tebby Otieno’s article highlights families ...

item.title: Eritrean Players Disappear After AFCON Qualifying Success
item.description: Ah, this one na serious matter! So, the Eritrea national team, dem get victory for their AFCON 2027 preliminary qualifiers against Eswatini – dem win 2-0 for the second leg, making the aggregate score...

item.title: Duffy prepares for public return after revealing traumatic past and legal name change
item.description: Welsh singer Duffy, famed for hits like 'Warwick Avenue' and 'Mercy', is set to return to the public eye after a long hiatus. Her absence followed a traumatic ordeal in 2010, when she was drugged, abd...

item.title: Steady improvements continue in Mkinga's 35.4bn shillings water supply project
item.description: The Tanzanian Government has reported significant progress in the ongoing Mkinga water project, valued at 35.4 billion Tanzanian shillings. The project, which aims to improve access to clean and safe ...

item.title: Sixteen Regional Parties Set to Compete in BARMM September 2026 Parliamentary Elections
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Sixteen regional political parties have announced plans to field candidates for the first-ever parliamentary elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), s...

item.title: Dozens killed in attacks by suspected jihadists in northwest Nigeria
item.description: In northwest Nigeria, more than 40 people have been killed in a series of violent attacks across at least nine villages in Kebbi and Niger states, according to local clergy. The raids began late Sunda...
